1.author: Ahmed Ali
1.text: OF WHAT ARE they asking one another?

1.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
1.text: What are they questioning each other about?

1.author: Arberry
1.text: Of what do they question one another?

1.author: Itani
1.text: What are they asking one another about?

1.author: Maududi
1.text: About what are they asking one another?

1.author: Pickthall
1.text: Whereof do they question one another?

1.author: Qarai
1.text: What is it that they are questioning each other about?!

1.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
1.text: What do they question each other about!

1.author: Saheeh International
1.text: About what are they asking one another?

1.author: Shakir
1.text: Of what do they ask one another?

1.author: Wahiduddin Khan
1.text: What are they asking each other about?

2.author: Hilali & Khan
2.text: About the great news, (i.e. Islamic Monotheism, the Quran, which Prophet Muhammad (Peace be upon him) brought and the Day of Resurrection, etc.),

2.author: Maududi
2.text: Is it about the awesome tiding

2.author: Sarwar
2.text: They quarrel about the great news

3.author: Ahmed Ali
3.text: About which they have been differing?

3.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
3.text: Regarding which they hold different views.

3.author: Hilali & Khan
3.text: About which they are in disagreement.

3.author: Maududi
3.text: that they are in utter disagreement?

3.author: Mubarakpuri
3.text: About which they are in disagreement.

3.author: Pickthall
3.text: Concerning which they are in disagreement.

3.author: Qarai
3.text: the one about which they differ?

3.author: Saheeh International
3.text: That over which they are in disagreement.

3.author: Wahiduddin Khan
3.text: concerning which they are in disagreement!

4.author: Ahmed Ali
4.text: Indeed they will come to know soon;

4.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
4.text: Surely yes, they will soon come to know!

4.author: Arberry
4.text: No indeed; they shall soon know!

4.author: Daryabadi
4.text: By no means! anon they shall know.

4.author: Hilali & Khan
4.text: Nay, they will come to know!

4.author: Maududi
4.text: No indeed; soon will they come to know;

4.author: Mubarakpuri
4.text: Nay, they will come to know!

4.author: Pickthall
4.text: Nay, but they will come to know!

4.author: Qarai
4.text: No indeed! Soon they will know!

4.author: Saheeh International
4.text: No! They are going to know.

4.author: Sarwar
4.text: (What they think is certainly despicable!). They will soon come to know (the reality).

4.author: Shakir
4.text: Nay! they shall soon come to know

4.author: Wahiduddin Khan
4.text: But they will soon come to know.

4.author: Yusuf Ali
4.text: Verily, they shall soon (come to) know!

5.author: Ahmed Ali
5.text: They will indeed come to know soon.

5.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
5.text: Again surely yes, they will soon come to know!

5.author: Arberry
5.text: Again, no indeed; they shall soon know!

5.author: Daryabadi
5.text: Again, by no means! anon they shall know.

5.author: Hilali & Khan
5.text: Nay, again, they will come to know!

5.author: Itani
5.text: Most certainly, they will find out.

5.author: Maududi
5.text: again, no indeed; soon will they come to know.

5.author: Mubarakpuri
5.text: Nay, again, they will come to know!

5.author: Pickthall
5.text: Nay, again, but they will come to know!

5.author: Qarai
5.text: No indeed! Soon they will know for once again!

5.author: Saheeh International
5.text: Then, no! They are going to know.

5.author: Sarwar
5.text: Yes, indeed, before long they will learn all about it.

5.author: Shakir
5.text: Nay! Nay! they shall soon know.

5.author: Wahiduddin Khan
5.text: Surely, they will soon find out the truth!

5.author: Yusuf Ali
5.text: Verily, verily they shall soon (come to) know!

6.author: Ahmed Ali
6.text: Did We not make the earth a spreading,

6.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
6.text: Did We not make the earth a bed?

6.author: Arberry
6.text: Have We not made the earth as a cradle

6.author: Daryabadi
6.text: Have We not made the earth an expanse.

6.author: Hilali & Khan
6.text: Have We not made the earth as a bed,

6.author: Itani
6.text: Did We not make the earth a cradle?

6.author: Maududi
6.text: Have We not spread the earth like a bed,

6.author: Mubarakpuri
6.text: Have We not made the earth as a bed,

6.author: Pickthall
6.text: Have We not made the earth an expanse,

6.author: Qarai
6.text: Did We not make the earth a resting place?

6.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
6.text: Have We not made the earth as a cradle

6.author: Saheeh International
6.text: Have We not made the earth a resting place?

6.author: Sarwar
6.text: Have We not made the earth as a place to rest

6.author: Shakir
6.text: Have We not made the earth an even expanse?

6.author: Wahiduddin Khan
6.text: Have We not spread the earth like a bed,

6.author: Yusuf Ali
6.text: Have We not made the earth as a wide expanse,

7.author: Maududi
7.text: and fixed the mountains like pegs,

7.author: Sarwar
7.text: and the mountains as pegs (to anchor the earth)?

7.author: Wahiduddin Khan
7.text: and raised the mountains like supporting poles?

8.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
8.text: And it is We who created you in pairs.

8.author: Arberry
8.text: And We created you in pairs,

8.author: Daryabadi
8.text: And We have created you in pairs.

8.author: Hilali & Khan
8.text: And We have created you in pairs (male and female, tall and short, good and bad, etc.).

8.author: Maududi
8.text: and created you in pairs (as men and women),

8.author: Mubarakpuri
8.text: And We have created you in pairs.

8.author: Pickthall
8.text: And We have created you in pairs,

8.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
8.text: And We created you in pairs,

8.author: Saheeh International
8.text: And We created you in pairs

8.author: Sarwar
8.text: Have We not created you in pairs,

8.author: Shakir
8.text: And We created you in pairs,

8.author: Yusuf Ali
8.text: And (have We not) created you in pairs,

9.author: Ahmed Ali
9.text: And made sleep for you to rest,

9.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
9.text: And made your sleep a rest.

9.author: Arberry
9.text: and We appointed your sleep for a rest;

9.author: Daryabadi
9.text: And We have made your sleep as a rest.

9.author: Hilali & Khan
9.text: And have made your sleep as a thing for rest.

9.author: Itani
9.text: And made your sleep for rest?

9.author: Maududi
9.text: and made your sleep a means of repose,

9.author: Mubarakpuri
9.text: And We have made your sleep as a thing for rest.

9.author: Pickthall
9.text: And have appointed your sleep for repose,

9.author: Qarai
9.text: and make your sleep for rest?

9.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
9.text: and We made your sleep a rest,

9.author: Saheeh International
9.text: And made your sleep [a means for] rest

9.author: Sarwar
9.text: made sleep for you to rest,

9.author: Shakir
9.text: And We made your sleep to be rest (to you),

9.author: Wahiduddin Khan
9.text: and gave you repose in sleep,

9.author: Yusuf Ali
9.text: And made your sleep for rest,

10.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
10.text: And made the night a cover.

10.author: Arberry
10.text: and We appointed night for a garment,

10.author: Daryabadi
10.text: And We have made the night a covering.

10.author: Hilali & Khan
10.text: And have made the night as a covering (through its darkness),

10.author: Itani
10.text: And made the night a cover?

10.author: Maududi
10.text: and made the night a covering,

10.author: Mubarakpuri
10.text: And We have made the night as a covering,

10.author: Pickthall
10.text: And have appointed the night as a cloak,

10.author: Qarai
10.text: and make the night a covering?

10.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
10.text: and We made the night a mantle,

10.author: Saheeh International
10.text: And made the night as clothing

10.author: Sarwar
10.text: made the night as a covering,

10.author: Shakir
10.text: And We made the night to be a covering,

10.author: Wahiduddin Khan
10.text: and the night as a cover,

10.author: Yusuf Ali
10.text: And made the night as a covering,

11.author: Ahmed Ali
11.text: And the day for seeking livelihood.

11.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
11.text: And made the day for seeking livelihood.

11.author: Arberry
11.text: and We appointed day for a livelihood.

11.author: Daryabadi
11.text: And We have made the day for seeking livelihood.

11.author: Hilali & Khan
11.text: And have made the day for livelihood.

11.author: Itani
11.text: And made the day for livelihood?

11.author: Maududi
11.text: and made the day to seek livelihood,

11.author: Mubarakpuri
11.text: And We have made the day for livelihood.

11.author: Pickthall
11.text: And have appointed the day for livelihood.

11.author: Qarai
11.text: and make the day for livelihood?

11.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
11.text: and We made the day for a livelihood.

11.author: Saheeh International
11.text: And made the day for livelihood

11.author: Sarwar
11.text: and the day as time for you to make a living?

11.author: Shakir
11.text: And We made the day for seeking livelihood.

11.author: Wahiduddin Khan
11.text: and made the day for earning a livelihood.

11.author: Yusuf Ali
11.text: And made the day as a means of subsistence?

12.author: Ahmed Ali
12.text: We raised over you several secure (skies),

12.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
12.text: And built seven strong roofs above you.

12.author: Arberry
12.text: And We have built above you seven strong ones,

12.author: Daryabadi
12.text: And We have builded over you seven strong heavens.

12.author: Hilali & Khan
12.text: And We have built above you seven strong (heavens),

12.author: Itani
12.text: And built above you seven strong ones?

12.author: Maududi
12.text: and built above you seven strong firmaments,

12.author: Mubarakpuri
12.text: And We have built above you seven strong,

12.author: Pickthall
12.text: And We have built above you seven strong (heavens),

12.author: Qarai
12.text: and build above you the seven mighty heavens?

12.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
12.text: And We built above you seven strong ones,

12.author: Saheeh International
12.text: And constructed above you seven strong [heavens]

12.author: Sarwar
12.text: Have We not made seven strong heavens above you,

12.author: Shakir
12.text: And We made above you seven strong ones,

12.author: Wahiduddin Khan
12.text: We have built above you seven mighty heavens,

12.author: Yusuf Ali
12.text: And (have We not) built over you the seven firmaments,

13.author: Ahmed Ali
13.text: And placed a lamp therein brightly burning.

13.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
13.text: And have kept a very bright lamp in it.

13.author: Arberry
13.text: and We appointed a blazing lamp

13.author: Daryabadi
13.text: And We have set therein lamps glowing.

13.author: Hilali & Khan
13.text: And have made (therein) a shinning lamp (sun).

13.author: Maududi
13.text: and placed therein a hot, shining lamp,

13.author: Mubarakpuri
13.text: And We have made (therein) a shining lamp.

13.author: Pickthall
13.text: And have appointed a dazzling lamp,

13.author: Qarai
13.text: and make [the sun for] a radiant lamp?

13.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
13.text: and placed in them a blazing lamp

13.author: Shakir
13.text: And We made a shining lamp,

13.author: Wahiduddin Khan
13.text: and We have set therein a glowing lamp.

13.author: Yusuf Ali
13.text: And placed (therein) a Light of Splendour?

14.author: Ahmed Ali
14.text: And We sent down rain from water-laden clouds pouring in torrents

14.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
14.text: And then sent down hard rain from the water bearing clouds.

14.author: Arberry
14.text: and have sent down out of the rainclouds water cascading

14.author: Daryabadi
14.text: And We have sent down from the rain-clouds water plenteous.

14.author: Hilali & Khan
14.text: And have sent down from the rainy clouds abundant water.

14.author: Itani
14.text: And brought down from the clouds pouring water?

14.author: Maududi
14.text: and sent down abundant water from the clouds

14.author: Mubarakpuri
14.text: And We have sent down from the Mu`sirat water Thajjaj.

14.author: Pickthall
14.text: And have sent down from the rainy clouds abundant water,

14.author: Qarai
14.text: and send down water pouring from the rain-clouds,

14.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
14.text: and have sent down from the clouds, pouring rain

14.author: Saheeh International
14.text: And sent down, from the rain clouds, pouring water

14.author: Sarwar
14.text: and sent down heavy rains from the clouds

14.author: Shakir
14.text: And We send down from the clouds water pouring forth abundantly,

14.author: Wahiduddin Khan
14.text: >From the rain clouds We send waters pouring down in abundance,

14.author: Yusuf Ali
14.text: And do We not send down from the clouds water in abundance,

15.author: Ahmed Ali
15.text: That We may produce from it grain and vegetation,

15.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
15.text: In order to produce grain and plants with it.

15.author: Arberry
15.text: that We may bring forth thereby grain and plants,

15.author: Daryabadi
15.text: So that We bring forth thereby corn and vegetation.

15.author: Hilali & Khan
15.text: That We may produce therewith corn and vegetations,

15.author: Itani
15.text: To produce with it grains and vegetation?

15.author: Maududi
15.text: so that We may thereby bring forth grain and vegetation,

15.author: Mubarakpuri
15.text: That We may produce therewith grains and vegetations,

15.author: Pickthall
15.text: Thereby to produce grain and plant,

15.author: Qarai
15.text: that We may bring forth with it grains and plants,

15.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
15.text: that We may bring forth with it grain, plants

15.author: Saheeh International
15.text: That We may bring forth thereby grain and vegetation

15.author: Shakir
15.text: That We may bring forth thereby corn and herbs,

15.author: Wahiduddin Khan
15.text: so that We may bring forth thereby grain and a variety of plants,

15.author: Yusuf Ali
15.text: That We may produce therewith corn and vegetables,

16.author: Ahmed Ali
16.text: And orchards full of trees and interlacing boughs.

17.author: Ahmed Ali
17.text: Surely a time is fixed for the Day of Judgement.

17.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
17.text: Indeed the Day of Decision is a time fixed.

17.author: Arberry
17.text: Surely the Day of Decision is an appointed time,

17.author: Daryabadi
17.text: Verily the Day of Decision is a time appointed.

17.author: Hilali & Khan
17.text: Verily, the Day of Decision is a fixed time,

17.author: Itani
17.text: The Day of Sorting has been appointed.

17.author: Maududi
17.text: Surely the Day of Judgement has an appointed time;

17.author: Mubarakpuri
17.text: Verily, the Day of Decision is a fixed time,

17.author: Pickthall
17.text: Lo! the Day of Decision is a fixed time,

17.author: Qarai
17.text: Indeed the Day of Judgement is the tryst,

17.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
17.text: Indeed, the Day of Decision is an appointed time,

17.author: Saheeh International
17.text: Indeed, the Day of Judgement is an appointed time -

17.author: Sarwar
17.text: The Day of Judgment will certainly be the final appointment.

17.author: Shakir
17.text: Surely the day of decision is (a day) appointed:

17.author: Wahiduddin Khan
17.text: Surely, the Day of Judgement has an appointed time.

17.author: Yusuf Ali
17.text: Verily the Day of Sorting out is a thing appointed,

18.author: Ahmed Ali
18.text: The day the trumpet blast is sounded you will come in hordes;

18.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
18.text: The day when the Trumpet will be blown – you will therefore come forth in multitudes.

18.author: Arberry
18.text: the day the Trumpet is blown, and you shall come in troops,

18.author: Daryabadi
18.text: It is a Day whereon the trumpet will be blown, and ye will come in multitudes.

18.author: Hilali & Khan
18.text: The Day when the Trumpet will be blown, and you shall come forth in crowds (groups);

18.author: Itani
18.text: The Day when the Trumpet is blown, and you will come in droves.

18.author: Maududi
18.text: the Day when the Trumpet shall be blown, and you will come forth in multitudes;

18.author: Mubarakpuri
18.text: The Day when the Trumpet will be blown, and you shall come forth in crowds.

18.author: Pickthall
18.text: A day when the trumpet is blown and ye come in multitudes,

18.author: Qarai
18.text: the day the Trumpet will be blown, and you will come in groups,

18.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
18.text: the Day that the Horn is blown, and you shall come in crowds

18.author: Saheeh International
18.text: The Day the Horn is blown and you will come forth in multitudes

18.author: Sarwar
18.text: On that day the trumpet will be sounded and you will come (to Us) in huge groups.

18.author: Shakir
18.text: The day on which the trumpet shall be blown so you shall come forth in hosts,

18.author: Wahiduddin Khan
18.text: On that Day when the trumpet shall be sounded, you shall come in droves,

18.author: Yusuf Ali
18.text: The Day that the Trumpet shall be sounded, and ye shall come forth in crowds;

18.author: Transliteration
18.text: Yawma yunfakhu fee alssoori fatatoona afwajan

19.author: Ahmed Ali
19.text: The heavens will be opened wide and turn into so many doors,

19.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
19.text: And the heaven will be opened – it therefore becomes like gates.

19.author: Arberry
19.text: and heaven is opened, and become gates,

19.author: Daryabadi
19.text: And the heaven will have been opened, and it will have become doors.

19.author: Hilali & Khan
19.text: And the heaven shall be opened, and it will become as gates,

19.author: Itani
19.text: And the sky is opened up, and becomes gateways.

19.author: Maududi
19.text: and when the sky shall be opened up and will become all doors;

19.author: Mubarakpuri
19.text: And the heaven shall be opened, and it will become as gates.

19.author: Pickthall
19.text: And the heaven is opened and becometh as gates,

19.author: Qarai
19.text: and the sky will be opened and become gates,

19.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
19.text: and heaven is opened, having gates,

19.author: Saheeh International
19.text: And the heaven is opened and will become gateways

19.author: Sarwar
19.text: The heavens will have openings like doors.

19.author: Shakir
19.text: And the heaven shall be opened so that it shall be all openings,

19.author: Wahiduddin Khan
19.text: and the heaven shall be opened, and become gates,

19.author: Yusuf Ali
19.text: And the heavens shall be opened as if there were doors,

20.author: Ahmed Ali
20.text: The mountains put in motion turning into a mirage.

20.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
20.text: And the mountains will be moved – they will therefore become like mirages.

20.author: Arberry
20.text: and the mountains are set in motion, and become a vapour.

20.author: Daryabadi
20.text: And the mountains will have been removed away, and they will have become as mirage.

20.author: Hilali & Khan
20.text: And the mountains shall be moved away from their places and they will be as if they were a mirage.

20.author: Itani
20.text: And the mountains are set in motion, and become a mirage.

20.author: Maududi
20.text: and the mountains will be set in motion and become a mirage.

20.author: Mubarakpuri
20.text: And the mountains shall be moved away from their places and they will be as if they were a mirage.

20.author: Pickthall
20.text: And the hills are set in motion and become as a mirage.

20.author: Qarai
20.text: and the mountains will be set moving and become a mirage.

20.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
20.text: and the mountains move, and vaporize.

20.author: Saheeh International
20.text: And the mountains are removed and will be [but] a mirage.

20.author: Sarwar
20.text: The mountains will be driven away and become like mirages.

20.author: Shakir
20.text: And the mountains shall be moved off so that they shall remain a mere semblance.

20.author: Wahiduddin Khan
20.text: and the mountains shall be made to vanish, as if they had been a mirage.

20.author: Yusuf Ali
20.text: And the mountains shall vanish, as if they were a mirage.

21.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
21.text: Indeed hell is lying in ambush.

21.author: Arberry
21.text: Behold, Gehenna has become an ambush,

21.author: Daryabadi
21.text: Verily the Hell is an ambuscade:

21.author: Hilali & Khan
21.text: Truly, Hell is a place of ambush,

21.author: Maududi
21.text: Surely the Hell is an ambush,

21.author: Mubarakpuri
21.text: Truly, Hell is a place of ambush

21.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
21.text: Indeed, Gehenna (Hell) has become an ambush,

21.author: Saheeh International
21.text: Indeed, Hell has been lying in wait

21.author: Sarwar
21.text: Hell will lie in wait (for its prey).

21.author: Yusuf Ali
21.text: Truly Hell is as a place of ambush,

22.author: Hilali & Khan
22.text: A dwelling place for the Taghun (those who transgress the boundry limits set by Allah like polytheists, disbelievers in the Oneness of Allah, hyprocrites, sinners, criminals, etc.),

22.author: Mubarakpuri
22.text: A dwelling place for the Taghun,

22.author: Saheeh International
22.text: For the transgressors, a place of return,

22.author: Sarwar
22.text: It will be a place of return

22.author: Shakir
22.text: A place of resort for the inordinate,

22.author: Yusuf Ali
22.text: For the transgressors a place of destination:

23.author: Ahmed Ali
23.text: Where they will remain for aeons,

23.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
23.text: They will remain in it for ages.

23.author: Daryabadi
23.text: They will tarry therein for ages.

23.author: Hilali & Khan
23.text: They will abide therein for ages,

23.author: Itani
23.text: Where they will remain for eons.

23.author: Maududi
23.text: therein they shall abide for ages,

23.author: Pickthall
23.text: They will abide therein for ages.

23.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
23.text: there, they shall live for ages,

23.author: Saheeh International
23.text: In which they will remain for ages [unending].

23.author: Sarwar
23.text: for the rebellious ones and they will live therein for ages.

23.author: Wahiduddin Khan
23.text: where they shall remain for ages,

23.author: Yusuf Ali
23.text: They will dwell therein for ages.

24.author: Ahmed Ali
24.text: Finding neither sleep nor any thing to drink

24.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
24.text: They will neither taste anything cool in it, nor anything to drink.

24.author: Arberry
24.text: tasting therein neither coolness nor any drink

24.author: Daryabadi
24.text: They will not taste therein cool or any drink.

24.author: Hilali & Khan
24.text: Nothing cool shall they taste therein, nor any drink.

24.author: Itani
24.text: They will taste therein neither coolness, nor drink.

24.author: Maududi
24.text: they shall taste in it no coolness, nor any pleasant drink

24.author: Mubarakpuri
24.text: Nothing cool shall they taste therein, nor any drink.

24.author: Pickthall
24.text: Therein taste they neither coolness nor (any) drink

24.author: Qarai
24.text: tasting in it neither any coolness nor drink,

24.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
24.text: tasting neither coolness nor any drink

24.author: Saheeh International
24.text: They will not taste therein [any] coolness or drink

24.author: Sarwar
24.text: They will not feel cold nor taste any drink

24.author: Shakir
24.text: They shall not taste therein cool nor drink

24.author: Wahiduddin Khan
24.text: and where they will taste neither coolness nor any drink

24.author: Yusuf Ali
24.text: Nothing cool shall they taste therein, nor any drink,

24.author: Transliteration
24.text: La yathooqoona feeha bardan wala sharaban

25.author: Ahmed Ali
25.text: Except boiling water and benumbing cold:

25.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
25.text: Except hot boiling water and hot pus discharged from the people of hell.

25.author: Hilali & Khan
25.text: Except boiling water, and dirty wound discharges.

25.author: Itani
25.text: Except boiling water, and freezing hail.

25.author: Maududi
25.text: save boiling water and wash of the wounds;

25.author: Pickthall
25.text: Save boiling water and a paralysing cold:

25.author: Shakir
25.text: But boiling and intensely cold water,

25.author: Wahiduddin Khan
25.text: save boiling water and a stinking fluid --

25.author: Yusuf Ali
25.text: Save a boiling fluid and a fluid, dark, murky, intensely cold,

26.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
26.text: The reward to each is according to what he is.

26.author: Hilali & Khan
26.text: An exact recompense (according to their evil crimes).

26.author: Maududi
26.text: a befitting recompense for their deeds.

26.author: Mubarakpuri
26.text: An exact recompense (according to their evil crimes).

26.author: Sarwar
26.text: as a fitting recompense for their deeds.

27.author: Ahmed Ali
27.text: They were those who did not expect a reckoning,

27.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
27.text: Indeed they had no fear of the account.

27.author: Arberry
27.text: They indeed hoped not for a reckoning,

27.author: Daryabadi
27.text: Verily they were wont not to look for a reckoning.

27.author: Hilali & Khan
27.text: For verily, they used not to look for a reckoning.

27.author: Itani
27.text: For they were not anticipating any reckoning.

27.author: Maududi
27.text: For indeed they did not look forward to any reckoning,

27.author: Mubarakpuri
27.text: For verily, they used not to look for a reckoning.

27.author: Pickthall
27.text: For lo! they looked not for a reckoning;

27.author: Qarai
27.text: Indeed they did not expect any reckoning,

27.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
27.text: Indeed, they did not hope for a Reckoning,

27.author: Saheeh International
27.text: Indeed, they were not expecting an account

27.author: Sarwar
27.text: They did not expect such a Judgment

27.author: Shakir
27.text: Surely they feared not the account,

27.author: Wahiduddin Khan
27.text: for they never expected to be called to account,

27.author: Yusuf Ali
27.text: For that they used not to fear any account (for their deeds),

28.author: Ahmed Ali
28.text: And rejected Our signs as lies.

28.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
28.text: And they denied Our signs to the extreme.

28.author: Arberry
28.text: and they cried loud lies to Our signs;

28.author: Daryabadi
28.text: And they belied Our revelations with strong belying.

28.author: Hilali & Khan
28.text: But they belied Our Ayat (proofs, evidences, verses, lessons, signs, revelations, and that which Our Prophet (Peace be upon him) brought) completely.

28.author: Itani
28.text: And they denied Our signs utterly.

28.author: Maududi
28.text: and roundly denied Our Signs as false.

28.author: Mubarakpuri
28.text: But they denied Our Ayat Kidhdhaba.

28.author: Pickthall
28.text: They called Our revelations false with strong denial.

28.author: Qarai
28.text: and they denied Our signs mendaciously,

28.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
28.text: and they absolutely belied Our verses,

28.author: Saheeh International
28.text: And denied Our verses with [emphatic] denial.

28.author: Shakir
28.text: And called Our communications a lie, giving the lie (to the truth).

28.author: Wahiduddin Khan
28.text: and they rejected outright Our signs;

28.author: Yusuf Ali
28.text: But they (impudently) treated Our Signs as false.

29.author: Ahmed Ali
29.text: We have kept account of everything in a book.

29.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
29.text: And We have kept recorded everything in a Book, accounted for.

29.author: Arberry
29.text: and everything We have numbered in a Book.

29.author: Daryabadi
29.text: And everything We have recorded in a book.

29.author: Hilali & Khan
29.text: And all things We have recorded in a Book.

29.author: Itani
29.text: But We have enumerated everything in writing.

29.author: Maududi
29.text: And everything have We recorded in a Book.

29.author: Mubarakpuri
29.text: And all things We have recorded in a Book.

29.author: Pickthall
29.text: Everything have We recorded in a Book.

29.author: Qarai
29.text: and We have figured everything in a Book.

29.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
29.text: and everything We have numbered in a Book.

29.author: Saheeh International
29.text: But all things We have enumerated in writing.

29.author: Sarwar
29.text: However, We have recorded everything in a book.

29.author: Shakir
29.text: And We have recorded everything in a book,

29.author: Wahiduddin Khan
29.text: but We have recorded everything in a Book.

29.author: Yusuf Ali
29.text: And all things have We preserved on record.

30.author: Ahmed Ali
30.text: So taste (the fruit of what you sowed), for We shall add nothing but torment,

30.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
30.text: Therefore taste it now – We shall not increase anything for you except the punishment.

30.author: Arberry
30.text: 'Taste! We shall increase you not save in chastisement.'

30.author: Daryabadi
30.text: Taste therefore. We shall not increase you in aught but torment.

30.author: Hilali & Khan
30.text: So taste you (the results of your evil actions); no increase shall We give you, except in torment.

30.author: Itani
30.text: So taste! We will increase you only in suffering.

30.author: Maududi
30.text: So taste (the fruit of your deeds). We shall only increase your torment.

30.author: Mubarakpuri
30.text: So taste you. No increase shall We give you, except in torment.

30.author: Pickthall
30.text: So taste (of that which ye have earned). No increase do We give you save of torment.

30.author: Qarai
30.text: So [now] taste! We shall increase you in nothing but punishment!

30.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
30.text: 'Taste! We shall not increase you except in punishment'

30.author: Saheeh International
30.text: "So taste [the penalty], and never will We increase you except in torment."

30.author: Sarwar
30.text: (They will be told), "Suffer, We shall only increase the torment for you".

30.author: Shakir
30.text: So taste! for We will not add to you aught but chastisement.

30.author: Wahiduddin Khan
30.text: [So We shall say], "Taste, then, [the fruit of your evil doings,] for now We shall bestow on you nothing but more and more suffering!"

30.author: Yusuf Ali
30.text: "So taste ye (the fruits of your deeds); for no increase shall We grant you, except in Punishment."

31.author: Ahmed Ali
31.text: As for those who preserve themselves from evil and follow the straight path, there is attainment for them:

31.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
31.text: Indeed the place of success is for the pious.

31.author: Arberry
31.text: Surely for the godfearing awaits a place of security,

31.author: Daryabadi
31.text: Verily for the God-fearing is an achievement.

31.author: Hilali & Khan
31.text: Verily, for the Muttaqun, there will be a success (Paradise);

31.author: Itani
31.text: But for the righteous there is triumph.

31.author: Maududi
31.text: Surely the state of triumph awaits the God-fearing:

31.author: Mubarakpuri
31.text: Verily, for those who have Taqwa, there will be a success;

31.author: Pickthall
31.text: Lo! for the duteous is achievement -

31.author: Qarai
31.text: Indeed a triumph awaits the Godwary:

31.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
31.text: But, for the cautious a place of prosperity,

31.author: Saheeh International
31.text: Indeed, for the righteous is attainment -

31.author: Sarwar
31.text: The pious ones will be triumphant.

31.author: Shakir
31.text: Surely for those who guard (against evil) is achievement,

31.author: Wahiduddin Khan
31.text: As for those who are mindful of God, they shall surely triumph:

31.author: Yusuf Ali
31.text: Verily for the Righteous there will be a fulfilment of (the heart's) desires;

32.author: Sarwar
32.text: They will have gardens and vineyards,

32.author: Wahiduddin Khan
32.text: theirs shall be gardens and vineyards,

33.author: Ahmed Ali
33.text: And graceful maidens of the same age,

33.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
33.text: And maidens of a single age.

33.author: Arberry
33.text: and maidens with swelling breasts, like of age,

33.author: Daryabadi
33.text: And full-breasted maidens of equal age.

33.author: Hilali & Khan
33.text: And young full-breasted (mature) maidens of equal age;

33.author: Maududi
33.text: and youthful maidens of like age,

33.author: Pickthall
33.text: And voluptuous women of equal age;

33.author: Qarai
33.text: and buxom maidens of a like age,

33.author: Sarwar
33.text: maidens with pears-shaped breasts who are of equal age (to their spouses)

33.author: Shakir
33.text: And voluptuous women of equal age;

33.author: Wahiduddin Khan
33.text: and young maidens of equal age,

33.author: Yusuf Ali
33.text: And voluptuous women of equal age;

34.author: Yusuf Ali
34.text: And a cup full (to the brim).

35.author: Ahmed Ali
35.text: They will hear no blasphemies there or disavowals:

35.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
35.text: In which they shall neither hear lewd talk, nor any lie.

35.author: Arberry
35.text: Therein they shall hear no idle talk, no cry of lies,

35.author: Daryabadi
35.text: They will hear therein no babble nor falsehood:

35.author: Hilali & Khan
35.text: No Laghw (dirty, false, evil talk) shall they hear therein, nor lying;

35.author: Itani
35.text: They will hear therein neither gossip, nor lies.

35.author: Maududi
35.text: Therein they shall hear no idle talk, nor any falsehood;

35.author: Mubarakpuri
35.text: No Laghw shall they hear therein, nor lying;

35.author: Pickthall
35.text: There hear they never vain discourse, nor lying -

35.author: Qarai
35.text: Therein they shall hear neither vain talk nor lies

35.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
35.text: There, they shall neither hear idle talk, nor yet any falsehood,

35.author: Saheeh International
35.text: No ill speech will they hear therein or any falsehood -

35.author: Sarwar
35.text: They will not hear therein any unnecessary words or lies.

35.author: Shakir
35.text: They shall not hear therein any vain words nor lying.

35.author: Wahiduddin Khan
35.text: There they shall not hear any idle talk, or any untruth:

35.author: Yusuf Ali
35.text: No vanity shall they hear therein, nor Untruth:-

35.author: Transliteration
35.text: La yasmaAAoona feeha laghwan wala kiththaban

36.author: Ahmed Ali
36.text: A recompense from your Lord, a sufficient gift,

36.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
36.text: A reward from your Lord – a grossly sufficient bestowal.

36.author: Arberry
36.text: for a recompense from thy Lord, a gift, a reckoning,

36.author: Daryabadi
36.text: A recompense from thy Lord-a gift sufficient -

36.author: Hilali & Khan
36.text: A reward from your Lord, an ample calculated gift (according to the best of their good deeds).

36.author: Itani
36.text: A reward from your Lord, a fitting gift.

36.author: Maududi
36.text: a recompense from your Lord and an ample reward

36.author: Mubarakpuri
36.text: Rewarded from your Lord with a sufficient gift.

36.author: Pickthall
36.text: Requital from thy Lord - a gift in payment -

36.author: Qarai
36.text: —a reward and a sufficing bounty from your Lord,

36.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
36.text: a recompense from your Lord, a gift, a reckoning,

36.author: Saheeh International
36.text: [As] reward from your Lord, [a generous] gift [made due by] account,

36.author: Sarwar
36.text: This will be their reward from your Lord, a favor from Him and a recompense for their deeds.

36.author: Shakir
36.text: A reward from your Lord, a gift according to a reckoning:

36.author: Wahiduddin Khan
36.text: all this will be a recompense, a gift, that will suffice them, from your Lord,

36.author: Yusuf Ali
36.text: Recompense from thy Lord, a gift, (amply) sufficient,

37.author: Ahmed Ali
37.text: The Lord of the heavens and the earth, and all that lies between them, most benevolent, to whom none may dare address a word.

37.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
37.text: Lord of the heavens and the earth, and all whatever is between them – the Most Gracious – with Whom no one will have the right to talk.

37.author: Arberry
37.text: Lord of the heavens and earth, and all that between them is, the All-merciful of whom they have no power to speak.

37.author: Daryabadi
37.text: From the Lord of the heavens and the earth and of whatsoever is in bet-ween them, the Compassionate with Whom they can demand not audience.

37.author: Hilali & Khan
37.text: (From) the Lord of the heavens and the earth, and whatsoever is in between them, the Most Beneficent, none can dare to speak with Him (on the Day of Resurrection except after His Leave).

37.author: Itani
37.text: Lord of the heavens and the earth, and everything between them—The Most Merciful—none can argue with Him.

37.author: Maududi
37.text: from the Lord of the heavens and the earth and of that which is between them; the Most Merciful Lord before Whom none dare utter a word.

37.author: Mubarakpuri
37.text: The Lord of the heavens and the earth, and whatsoever is in between them, the Most Gracious, with Whom th- ey cannot dare to speak.

37.author: Pickthall
37.text: Lord of the heavens and the earth, and (all) that is between them, the Beneficent; with Whom none can converse.

37.author: Qarai
37.text: the All-beneficent, the Lord of the heavens and the earth and whatever is between them. They will not be able to address Him

37.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
37.text: the Lord of the heavens and earth, and all that is between them, the Merciful, of whom they are unable to speak.

37.author: Saheeh International
37.text: [From] the Lord of the heavens and the earth and whatever is between them, the Most Merciful. They possess not from Him [authority for] speech.

37.author: Sarwar
37.text: He is the Lord of the heavens and the earth and all that is between them. He is the Beneficent God and no one will be able to address Him.

37.author: Shakir
37.text: The Lord of the heavens and the earth and what is between them, the Beneficent Allah, they shall not be able to address Him.

37.author: Wahiduddin Khan
37.text: the Sustainer of the heavens and the earth and all that lies between them, the most Gracious [and] none shall have it in their power to raise their voices to Him.

37.author: Yusuf Ali
37.text: (From) the Lord of the heavens and the earth, and all between, (Allah) Most Gracious: None shall have power to argue with Him.

37.author: Transliteration
37.text: Rabbi alssamawati waalardi wama baynahuma alrrahmani la yamlikoona minhu khitaban

38.author: Ahmed Ali
38.text: The day the Spirit takes its stand, with the angels ranged in rows. None will speak except who is permitted by Ar-Rahman and says what is right.

38.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
38.text: The day when Jibreel and all the angels will stand in rows; none will be able to speak except one whom the Most Gracious commands and who spoke rightly. (Prophet Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him – will be the first to be granted permission to intercede.)

38.author: Arberry
38.text: Upon the day when the Spirit and the angels stand in ranks they shall speak not, save him to whom the All-merciful has given leave, and who speaks aright.

38.author: Daryabadi
38.text: On the Day whereon the spirits and the angels will stand arrayed, they will not be able to speak save him whom the Compassionate giveth leave and who speaketh aright.

38.author: Hilali & Khan
38.text: The Day that Ar-Ruh [Jibrael (Gabriel) or another angel] and the angels will stand forth in rows, none shall speak except him whom the Most Beneficent (Allah) allows, and he will speak what is right.

38.author: Itani
38.text: On the Day when the Spirit and the angels stand in row. They will not speak, unless it be one permitted by the Most Merciful, and he will say what is right.

38.author: Maududi
38.text: The Day when the Spirit and the angels are ranged row on row. None shall speak save he whom the Merciful Lord will permit; and he too will speak what is right.

38.author: Mubarakpuri
38.text: The Day that Ar-Ruh and the angels will stand forth in rows, they will not speak except him whom Ar-Rahman allows, and he will speak what is right.

38.author: Pickthall
38.text: On the day when the angels and the Spirit stand arrayed, they speak not, saving him whom the Beneficent alloweth and who speaketh right.

38.author: Qarai
38.text: on the day when the Spirit and the angels stand in an array: none shall speak except someone who is permitted by the All-beneficent and says what is rightful.

38.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
38.text: On that Day, when the Spirit (Gabriel) and the angels stand in ranks they shall not speak, except he to whom the Merciful has given permission, and sayswhat is right.

38.author: Saheeh International
38.text: The Day that the Spirit and the angels will stand in rows, they will not speak except for one whom the Most Merciful permits, and he will say what is correct.

38.author: Sarwar
38.text: On that day, the Spirit and the angels who stand in lines will not speak except those whom the Beneficent God has permitted, and he will speak the right words.

38.author: Shakir
38.text: The day on which the spirit and the angels shall stand in ranks; they shall not speak except he whom the Beneficent Allah permits and who speaks the right thing.

38.author: Wahiduddin Khan
38.text: On the Day when the Spirit and the angels stand in ranks, no one will speak, except for those to whom the Lord of Mercy gives permission, and who will say only what is right.

38.author: Yusuf Ali
38.text: The Day that the Spirit and the angels will stand forth in ranks, none shall speak except any who is permitted by (Allah) Most Gracious, and He will say what is right.

38.author: Transliteration
38.text: Yawma yaqoomu alrroohu waalmalaikatu saffan la yatakallamoona illa man athina lahu alrrahmanu waqala sawaban

39.author: Ahmed Ali
39.text: That day is certain. So whosoever likes may prepare a way to his Lord.

39.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
39.text: That is the True Day; therefore whoever wills may take the path towards his Lord.

39.author: Arberry
39.text: That is the true day; so whosoever wills takes unto his Lord a resort.

39.author: Daryabadi
39.text: That is the Sure Day. Whosoever therefore willeth, let him betake Unto his Lord a resort.

39.author: Hilali & Khan
39.text: That is without doubt the True Day, so, whosoever wills, let him seek a place with (or a way to) His Lord (by obeying Him in this worldly life)!

39.author: Itani
39.text: That is the Day of Reality. So whoever wills, let him take a way back to his Lord.

39.author: Maududi
39.text: That Day is sure to come. So let him who will seek a resort with his Lord.

39.author: Mubarakpuri
39.text: That is the True Day. So, whosoever wills, let him seek a place with His Lord!

39.author: Pickthall
39.text: That is the True Day. So whoso will should seek recourse unto his Lord.

39.author: Qarai
39.text: That day is true for certain. So let anyone who wishes take resort with his Lord.

39.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
39.text: That is the Day, the truth, so whosoever wills takes a way to his Lord.

39.author: Saheeh International
39.text: That is the True Day; so he who wills may take to his Lord a [way of] return.

39.author: Sarwar
39.text: That will be the Day of the Truth. So let those who want seek refuge from their Lord.

39.author: Shakir
39.text: That is the sure day, so whoever desires may take refuge with his Lord.

39.author: Wahiduddin Khan
39.text: That Day is sure to come, so whoever wishes to, let him take the path that leads towards his Lord.

39.author: Yusuf Ali
39.text: That Day will be the sure Reality: Therefore, whoso will, let him take a (straight) return to his Lord!

39.author: Transliteration
39.text: Thalika alyawmu alhaqqu faman shaa ittakhatha ila rabbihi maaban

40.author: Ahmed Ali
40.text: We have indeed warned you of a calamity near at hand, a day when man will see what he had sent (of his deeds) ahead, and the unbeliever will say: "Woe alas. Ah would that I were dust!"

40.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
40.text: We warn you of a punishment that has come near; a day on which man will see what his own hands have sent ahead, and the disbeliever will say, “Alas – if only I were dust!”

40.author: Arberry
40.text: Lo, We have warned you of a nigh chastisement, upon the day when a man shall behold what his hands have forwarded, and the unbeliever shall say, 'O would that I were dust!'

40.author: Daryabadi
40.text: Verily We! We have warned you of a torment nigh at hand, a Day whereon a man shall behold that which his hands have sent forth, and the infidel will say: would that I were dust!

40.author: Hilali & Khan
40.text: Verily, We have warned you of a near torment, the Day when man will see that (the deeds) which his hands have sent forth, and the disbeliever will say: "Woe to me! Would that I were dust!"

40.author: Itani
40.text: We have warned you of a near punishment—the Day when a person will observe what his hands have produced, and the faithless will say, “O, I wish I were dust.”

40.author: Maududi
40.text: Lo! We warn you of a chastisement near at hand; the Day when a man will look on what his own hands have sent forth, and the unbelievers shall say: “Oh would that I were utter dust.”

40.author: Mubarakpuri
40.text: Verily, We have warned you of a near torment -- the Day when man will see that which his hands have sent forth, and the disbeliever will say: "Woe to me! Would that I were dust!"

40.author: Pickthall
40.text: Lo! We warn you of a doom at hand, a day whereon a man will look on that which his own hands have sent before, and the disbeliever will cry: "Would that I were dust!"

40.author: Qarai
40.text: Indeed We have warned you of a punishment near at hand—the day when a person will observe what his hands have sent ahead and the faithless one will say, ‘I wish I were dust!’

40.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
40.text: Indeed, We have forewarned you of an imminent punishment, the Day when the person will look upon his works and the unbelievers will say: 'Would that I were dust'

40.author: Saheeh International
40.text: Indeed, We have warned you of a near punishment on the Day when a man will observe what his hands have put forth and the disbeliever will say, "Oh, I wish that I were dust!"

40.author: Sarwar
40.text: We have warned you of the approaching torment. On that day, a person will see what his hands have committed. A disbeliever will say, "Would that I had been dust".

40.author: Shakir
40.text: Surely We have warned you of a chastisement near at hand: the day when man shall see what his two hands have sent before, and the unbeliever shall say: O! would that I were dust!

40.author: Wahiduddin Khan
40.text: We have warned you of a chastisement which is near at hand, on the Day when man shall [clearly] see what his hands have sent ahead, and when he who has denied the truth shall say, "Oh, would that I were dust!"

40.author: Yusuf Ali
40.text: Verily, We have warned you of a Penalty near, the Day when man will see (the deeds) which his hands have sent forth, and the Unbeliever will say, "Woe unto me! Would that I were (metre) dust!"

40.author: Transliteration
40.text: Inna antharnakum AAathaban qareeban yawma yanthuru almaro ma qaddamat yadahu wayaqoolu alkafiru ya laytanee kuntu turaban
